---
title: "Ladybug PopGen -- Environment & Infrastructure Reference"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float:
      collapsed: true
      smooth_scroll: true
    toc_depth: 3
    theme: default
---

# Ladybug PopGen -- Environment & Infrastructure Reference

**Purpose:** Stable reference for platform details, environment setup, and version control configuration. Read on demand when working with infrastructure, setting up environments, or committing code.

**Last Updated:** 2026-04-13

---

# 1. Platform

**Local machine (current -- January 2026):**

- MacBook Pro (Mac16,1), model MCX04LL/A
- Apple M4, 10 cores (4 performance + 6 efficiency), 24 GB RAM
- Used for: R/Python scripting, pcadapt, OutFLANK, moderate VCF processing

**Cluster (original analysis platform, Nov-Dec 2025):**

- Jetstream2 (Indiana University) via ACCESS-CI grant BIO250306
- Ubuntu 24.04 LTS
- Used for: Initial analysis pipeline (diversity, FST, selection scans, BLAST)

**When to use which:**

| Task | Local (MacBook) | Cluster (Jetstream2) |
|---|---|---|
| pcadapt (~800K SNPs x 88 samples) | Yes (~15-20 min) | Not needed |
| OutFLANK | Yes (~13 min) | Not needed |
| VCF filtering (moderate files) | Yes | Large multi-sample VCFs |
| hapFLK (if ever needed) | No (memory-intensive) | Yes |
| BLAST against large databases | Slow | Yes |

---

# 2. Environment Setup

**All analysis scripts run within the `pop_gen` Conda environment.**

```bash
# Activate (interactive shell)
conda activate pop_gen

# Activate (non-interactive bash, e.g. Claude Code)
source /Users/reina/miniconda3/etc/profile.d/conda.sh && conda activate pop_gen

# Verify
which vcftools    # Should point to conda env
which Rscript     # Should point to conda env
which plink       # Should point to conda env
```

**Key dependencies:**

| Package | Version | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| vcftools | v0.1.16 | VCF manipulation, diversity stats, FST |
| PLINK | v1.9+ | Genotype format conversion, sample QC |
| R | v4.x | Statistical analysis, visualization |
| Python | 3.x | Annotation scripts, data processing |
| BLAST+ | v2.17.0 | Sequence similarity search |
| BioPython | -- | BLAST parsing, sequence handling |

**R packages:**

| Package | Purpose |
|---|---|
| OutFLANK | FST outlier detection (validation method) |
| pcadapt | PCA-based outlier detection |
| qvalue | FDR control for pcadapt (Bioconductor: `BiocManager::install("qvalue")`) |
| dplyr, tidyr | Data manipulation |
| ggplot2 | Visualization |
| data.table | Fast file I/O for large TSVs |
| ggnewscale | Dual color scales in Figure 1 |
| patchwork | Multi-panel plot composition |

**Known environment gaps:**

- bcftools is NOT installed in pop_gen (was needed for VCF merging but no longer required after PLINK approach adopted)

---

# 3. Version Control

**Remote:** `git@github.com:ReitheHeroine/BIOL624_Project_Fall_2025.git`

**Branch strategy:** `main` only (no feature branches unless discussed).

**What gets committed:**

- `scripts/*.sh`, `scripts/*.R`, `scripts/*.py`
- `README.md`, `METHODS.md`
- `project_notes/*.md` and `project_notes/*.Rmd` (text-based documentation)
- `metadata/popmap.txt`, `metadata/samples.tsv`
- `results/` summary files and plots

**What does NOT get committed** (handled by .gitignore):

- `data/` -- entire directory excluded (VCFs, PLINK files, reference genomes, BLAST databases)
- `*.log` -- machine-specific timestamped logs
- `.DS_Store`, `__pycache__/`, `*.pyc`

Note: The .gitignore is broad (excludes all of `data/`). Additional exclusions for `.Rhistory`, `.RData`, `literature/`, and `.docx` files are not currently in .gitignore but are not tracked.

**GitHub status:**

| Item | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Repository created | Done | `github.com:ReitheHeroine/BIOL624_Project_Fall_2025` |
| .gitignore configured | Done | Broad exclusions (data/, .DS_Store, __pycache__, *.log) |
| Initial commit | Done | Multiple commits on main branch |
| README updated | Not done | Currently minimal (3 lines) |

---

# 4. Resource Requirements

- **Storage:** ~50GB for VCF files, results, and intermediate files
- **Memory:** 16-32GB RAM for large VCF processing. pcadapt peak ~8 GB (within 24 GB local capacity).
- **CPU:** Multi-core beneficial for parallel chromosome processing
